All right, welcome back in this lesson. We are going to set the stage with openings and agenda settings. So The first three minutes of your call will really set the tone for how the rest of the enrollment Conversation is going to go. So if you can set the stage this show will follow I know that sounds cheesy, but it's true, right these first three minutes is really what's going to create the vibe create the energy Create the connection that's really going to make the rest of the call feel like there's some momentum behind it the safety the power and the connection to the vision all those things we spoke about back in module two So this is how we are going to break it down Right from the beginning the openings and the first words so that it's not this weird awkward Jumbled together mess we're gonna show you how to set the agenda How to get that first permission at that first critical juncture and then how to effortlessly and seamlessly transition into the full Situational gestalt. All right, so let's do it. So openings and first words All right, so they're going to orient around your tone, right? So it only goes to reason only stands to reason that you are going to want to presume a certain degree of familiarity and Relaxness give them the opportunity to orient around presumed familiarity and relaxness. So we do this by limiting Weird creepy cringy small talk or anything that could be disingenuous otherwise known as wasting verbal real estate and mental bandwidth on asking questions about things you really don't care about just To warm them up. You don't have to pretend you are interested in where they're living You don't have to pretend that you are a fan of their local sports team You don't have to do any of that and it's actually better that you don't right and if you are Slightly introverted or awkward like I am don't mask it right that could be a disarming quality It could actually give them a sense of feeling connected to you, right if they are somewhat introverted awkward Like you are right so don't mess that that's not something you need to overcome through disingenuous small talk Next this should be the easy fun Relaxed enjoyable part right expect express Genuine excitement around your opportunity to get to talk to them right if you have some sort of existing link or familiarity Right, you could say I've been seeing you show up on my feet and seeing all the awesome stuff You're sharing in the group I've heard amazing things from some common friends and I'm just really excited But we finally have a chance to connect if you've seen their name in your programs Right, maybe they were in one of your courses and now you're talking about a bigger opportunity say yeah I've seen your name show up in the course really grateful to have you in that environment and stoked that we finally get to connect So you could express genuine excitement You can just express genuine excitement around something that caught your eye in their application So you could say I had a chance to review it with my team yesterday And we all thought your mission to do X is so admirable I've were humbled and appreciative of the opportunity to serve you in that right it shows That you've invested care and attention in the story that they've invested to share with you Once again rule applies avoid Phony Rapport buildings so most will see through it It will create this defensiveness and distrust right off the bat Something you're going to have to work extra hard to overcome throughout the call But that said right there should be something that you're genuinely curious and excited about otherwise Why are you talking to them right there must be something about that application that got you excited? to allow that call to get booked to stay on your calendar and for you to invest your 45 50 60 minutes in talking to them So know what that is ahead of time and be willing to share that in your opening Exchanges with your pre-customer Cool when it comes to setting the agenda the agenda is all about establishing Safety and power so if we give them a future pace of exactly what's going to happen In the next 20 to 50 minutes all of a sudden it's less Threatening right and if we give them the choice to proceed or not to proceed via permission via consent That is then exercising their power So a lot is going on just in that minute or two that you're setting the agenda So if your pre-call process has made it clear that this is about exploring their candidacy or qualification in a program this whole part about the agenda setting should be quite easy and Natural and as I said I do recommend that your pre-call process Either how they booked the call or the messages they've had in the time between booking and getting on the call It should be abundantly clear everywhere right that you are going to be exploring their candidacy for this opportunity So the agenda should be short It should be digestible and it must be in line with what they're already Expecting from that pre-call process that you walk them through So this might look as simple as so my goal is to help us both get clear and the best way to partner together And what I'd like to do to arrive at that is really unpack as much around your current situation as possible Or I want to get a bit more clear about the challenges you're experiencing in your relationship Why you feel that's showing up and where you'd like to see it evolve And then if I feel like there's a strong path forward together will explore those options So this agenda setting is really short another one I'd love to hear about what's really calling you to this opportunity Where and how you see it fitting in and he concerns you may have and if it feels good from there We can talk about the best path forward another version I want to really dive deeper into what inspired you to jump on this call explore what you're up to in your course business And then if things are feeling alive, we can go over the full details of what's inside automated intimacy Then finally, I'd love to start off by going through your experience in this seven-day challenge We just ran what results you've had any challenges that have come up along the way And if we each feel it's a great fit we'll walk through the best way to get started together So this is something you may set as the agenda if they came in from the challenge Next getting permission so after you've set the agenda Simply ask for buy-in right is that comfortable? Does that feel comfortable? How is this feeling for you right and give them a chance to give an honest answer on that? So the goal here is to anchor in and affirm safety and comfort right and power in being given and exercising that choice and permission So when asking for permission because we want it to be real We want them to anchor in and affirm their safety and comfort We want to avoid the typical does that sound good because if it feels Rhetorical and the after effect of them saying yeah sounds good. Let's go if the after effect is feeling that they didn't Truly express their will or their power but gave an automatic forced Disempowered or conditioned yes, which really is compliance then we haven't Given them a chance to actually answer powerfully We haven't actually given them the sense of safety that this permission this consent is here to achieve You can also combine your personal goal in that permission right so you could say my goal For this beta isn't to maximize revenue But to get the right 20 people into this program and right Meaning really in a position to take these advanced scaling methods and make the most out of them So if it's a fit we'll do what we can to make the investment feel comfortable. How does that feel right? So you can state your goal beforehand. It's not selfish It's not out of context this whole illusion that it has to be solely focused everything only focus on your prospect Actually makes the situation a little bit more threatening, right? You are allowed to have a goal and you're allowed to share it and in many ways That's actually what helps your prospect warm up and become more forthcoming in sharing with you Next you're going to want to transition into the Gestalt. So this is once again a very short Short part of that first part of the sales call So this could be the inciting incident or the point of no return open or a combination of both So let's look at what these look like so the inciting incident this will help them connect to what's already Resonated about you your content your program essentially what got them on this call today and it'll have them beginning Begin exploring sorry elements of why they feel there's relevancy why they believe there may be a match Why they believe there may be a fit between what you do and what they're experiencing And then finally if you begin openly it if they begin by openly sharing a tipping point moment in their life or business That's motivating them. They're all of a sudden going to be connected to their power and resourcefulness Right off the bat. So what this looks like as a question is simple It's cool. Well floor is yours and I'd really love to simply hear what inspired you to get on this call Right and learn more about X program not made you jump on this call. That's compliance language But what inspired you right? We want to know what type of inspiration they're feeling what happened in their life What was that tipping point moment? What was it about your content that resonated or the words on your sales page? Essentially, why are they here and that's often the most honest and best way to get started Next one there could be the point of no return open So if you gave them some Pre-call homework whether that was in the form of an assessment a quiz a challenge Mini course whatever it was where you were tracking Metrics or asking them to track certain metrics within it. That could be a great starting point, right? It becomes a very easy question I'd love to start off by hearing more about your experience in the power hour challenge So this is one run by my good friend myel Stutz Another one I'd love to know how you were feeling after the five-day juice cleanse that we all just did together Or I'd love to know what results you got from the five-day email writing boot camp So it starts them off from the place They left off in either the challenge or the pre-call environment that they were in before and it makes for a super super Interesting opening where you actually get to start on a really great vibe Next you can also ask questions on your application That could be great jumping off points So this is something to think about when you're writing through the application form that they're going to sign up for what can you ask in there? That's going to make it easier more enjoyable for you to reflect something back as you proceed into the opening of the call So you could ask what's the title of the nonfiction book you're currently working on right? How awesome would it be if you are a book writing coach? If you know that title beforehand right and can ask them immediately alright So tell me how it's going with X title right and that is to lead in into unpacking the distal What song are you currently trying to learn on your guitar another amazing thing like how's it going learning? I don't know sweet child of mine that opening riff must be really freaking hard Do you have any active Instagram ads running right now if they say yes on that application if they talk about what that? Current set looks like you could ask them right off the bat in that call leading into the main gestalt So tell me about how this specific ad set is going So what you ask on your application what you do in your pre-call environment is really what's going to make the call itself and the lead-in into This gestalt so much more easier so much more seamless and so much more natural without having to rely on any of these stuffy weird artificial raport building techniques and when you do this unpack the full dimensionality of the wind so if you are getting some degree of feedback right on How their experience was in this challenge setting whether that's a five-day juice cleanse a five-day email writing bootcamp power hour challenge Or even a song they're working on or ads they're running Get them to experience the dimensionality of the wind You know how did it feel being able to do X? This is what's going to help you anchor in that power that relief that sense of achievability What do you think that means for you going forward right dimensionality means? What are the future? Possibilities now available. How have they rewritten new? Possibilities in their life based on what they just achieved now Then of course can you see yourself continuing to do this if they just came in from a challenge and they're Indigorated by these results that your program will help them amplify and get more of Getting this kind of commitment is going to be so crucial right? Can you see yourself continuing to do this? This is going to create consistency past that point of no return where there is less friction Continuing on into your program for them to continue doing it and doing more of it and doing it better Then backtracking back into this world before they ever came across your stuff So that is what is going on in the first three five seven ten minutes of your call It's made easier simpler and more effective when you really weave it in to the pre call experience This is something we're going to be doing more training throughout on something called application funnel amplifiers So check that out if it's available But other than that this should give you a good starting point to really start these calls off on the right note Awkward and all but not cringy, which is really what we're going for So that's what we got for you next up We are going to go deep into unpacking the situational Distalt take a breather take a sip of water and I will catch you there
